questions.question: What's the actual impact of fees and slippage on retail-size paper-traded crypto strategies?
questions.summary: ~$1/round-trip on $200 positions (0.25% taker both legs). Gross +$1,085 became net -$782 on the 3-month backtest — the delta is the answer. Fees erase thin edges.

questions.question: Which parameter ranges of Bollinger and MA-crossover are robust to regime shifts, and which are overfit?
questions.summary: None are robust: all 12 variants with n>100 negative over 6 months net of costs. Per-regime conditioning untested (rung 3).

questions.question: (Emergent) Do constraints or strategies determine outcomes at this scale?
questions.summary: Constraints dominate: 99.6% of candidate signals died on cooldown/exposure/concurrency. Candidate fitness = edge per constraint slot, not signal frequency.

questions.question: Does sentiment data (Fear & Greed) improve technical strategies, or is it noise?
questions.summary: Layer 2 (context) never went live; deferred past Phase 1b per one-factor-at-a-time.

questions.question: Does the walk-forward tuner produce variants that hold up out-of-sample?
questions.summary: tune.py never built; compare.py (the scoring instrument) now exists. Blocked on the candidate roster accumulating live trades.

learnings.claim: GitHub Actions free-tier */5 cron on small/inactive public repos delivers ~24 runs/day (~3-5% of the documented 288/day), not the assumed 5-minute cadence.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-18 GH cron cadence entry; confirmed by 2026-05-23 migration entry (69 of 1,712 expected = 4.0% invocation rate)

learnings.claim: GitHub delays then drops scheduled triggers infrastructure-side (runs scatter across minutes-of-hour rather than clustering at multiples of 5), not because of a config error.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-23 migration entry, config audit clean and scatter diagnostic

learnings.claim: Migrating the cron to a Hetzner CX22 VPS running system cron makes the 95% uptime gate achievable as written without amending Phase 2 entry criteria.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 47-day-gap entry: VPS produced 11,490+ fetch commits with zero >1h gaps, runs 84->11,574 all status='ok', satisfying Phase 2 gate 1

learnings.claim: A monitoring/infrastructure system running cleanly is not a substitute for the operator showing up — the VPS ran 47 days clean while the curriculum did not move at all.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 47-day-gap entry: signals/trades/decisions/llm_calls still 0, STRATEGY_VARIANTS still {}, no Friday review since W21

learnings.claim: The 2026-05-23 review's bear case that 'the infrastructure was the project' was confirmed by 47 additional days of the VPS running while zero trades/decisions were produced.
Both Phase 1 base strategies (Bollinger mean-reversion and MA-crossover) lose money over a realistic 6-month window; every variant with a real sample (n>100) is negative.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 retire-Bollinger-and-MA entry: 12 variants, 6-month replay, net -$1,719.44, bollinger_default n=539 -$636.75

learnings.claim: The only positive base-strategy variants (macross_slow n=3, macross_default n=13) are noise, not signal, because their sample sizes are far below the significance threshold.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 retire-Bollinger-and-MA entry; contrasted against bollinger_default n=539 negative

learnings.claim: Constraints (cooldown/exposure/concurrency) dominate strategy: 99.6% of candidate signals are rejected before execution.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 retire entry (1,371 trades / 390,519 candidates) and 2026-07-02 front-loaded-variants entry

learnings.claim: bollinger_loose (sigma=2.5) is too wide in every regime, not just one — it fired zero times across all six months.
Running all 12 variants in parallel on Day 1 adds no risk because the shared $1,000 position ceiling means variants compete for the same cap rather than adding exposure.
learnings.evidence: 2026-07-02 front-loaded-variants entry: total exposure never breached; adding variants moved P&L only through selection within the cap

learnings.claim: Deterministic hash-based signal generation (hash of symbol+bar_timestamp) plus the signals table UNIQUE constraint makes each cron cycle idempotent, whereas random() would double-signal on re-runs and break backtest reproducibility.
Without Layer 4 exits (stop/target/24h time exit), the 5-position cap deadlocks the loop after 5 trades.

No real credential was ever committed to the repo — every .env.template version contains only placeholders and .env was never tracked; the .env exposure surface was the Claude Code session transcript.
Deferring an incident write-up loses the facts that matter in a postmortem — this record was written ~60 days late from memory and the exact date/command/transcript persistence are gone.
A VPS hiccup is a bounded, visible failure (logged status='failed', data resumes) whereas a missing GitHub Actions invocation is invisible with no audit trail.
learnings.evidence: 2026-05-23 migration entry, letter to future self

learnings.claim: At ~10 trades/day per variant (~300 data points/month) the sample is far too few to train a useful model — any model appearing to work on 300 points is overfitting.
$1,000 vs $200 capital is mostly psychological; what $1,000 buys is experimental longevity — a 30% drawdown leaves $700 (survivable) vs $140 (out of the experiment).
At the project's scale (~10 trades/day per variant) one-factor-at-a-time yields more learnable results than a factorial design, which needs large samples to populate its cells.
The epistemic infrastructure produced a clean, defensible negative when the signal wasn't there, which was the actual experimental goal (not whether Bollinger makes money).
If null_baseline shows a positive edge over a real sample, the simulator, fee model, or market data is wrong — expected P&L for random entries is slowly negative due to fees.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of the first calendar week of cron operation, the runs table should contain ~2,016 rows with status='ok' (90%+ success rate); if below 90%, investigate before Week 2 strategy-roster review begins.
hypotheses.resolution: Amended by the 2026-05-18 and 2026-05-23 entries: GitHub Actions delivered only ~4% invocation rate (69 of 1,712), far below the assumed cadence, so the original 90%-success hypothesis was unfalsifiable and was replaced by separate invocation-rate and code-success-rate metrics.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, the operator commits a decision-log entry naming one of (a) keep GH cron for fetch+signals, move execute to real scheduler before Phase 2; (b) move signals+execute together to one scheduler now; (c) move everything to one real scheduler before Week 3. If not committed by end of Week 2, the Week 3 build does not begin until it's made.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-18 — GH cron cadence does not match assumed 5-min
hypotheses.resolution: Resolved by the 2026-05-23 migration decision, which moved the cron to a Hetzner VPS running the existing fetch.py.

hypotheses.commitment: By end of Week 2, Claude-mediated synthesis will produce either (a) a defensible justification for keeping Bollinger and MA-crossover naming which modern-filter criteria they pass/fail, or (b) a defensible alternative roster with the same justification structure. If neither is achievable, Week 3 starts a postmortem on the reframe itself.
hypotheses.resolution: The 2026-07-02 roster call retired both base strategies on 6-month evidence rather than at end of Week 2; the resolution came from a real replay window, not a Week-2 modern-filter justification, and the deadline was long past.

hypotheses.commitment: If neither base strategy clears the bar over a real window, replace them (the reframe's falsifiable hypothesis on the base strategies).
hypotheses.resolution: 2026-07-02: the 6-month replay showed every real-sample variant negative; both base strategies retired from the active roster.

hypotheses.commitment: Original 2026-05-17 hypothesis: 90%+ success rate by end of first calendar week of GitHub-Actions-invoked runs.
hypotheses.source_entry: 2026-05-23 — Migrating cron from GitHub Actions to Hetzner VPS (amendment)
hypotheses.resolution: Amended into two separately tracked metrics — invocation rate (VPS-controlled, target >=99%) and code success rate (project-controlled, target >=95%) — combining to 95%+ uptime of expected runs over any 4-week window, matching the Phase 2 architectural gate; this removed the platform-induced ceiling making the original hypothesis unfalsifiable.
